message:
test(connectors): add C3.4 constraints_cbor to apple/email/wecom/browser token builders

Continue the C3.4 default-deny CapabilityConstraints sweep begun in
earlier commits 009900b8 and 7c92ea08, now covering the last five
connectors whose unit-test token builders were still producing tokens
without the required CapabilityConstraints block.

Each connector gains:
- Cargo.toml: ciborium dev-dependency (for constraints CBOR encoding
  in tests); browser already had the dep so only src changes
- src/connector.rs: import CapabilityConstraints, add a
  test_constraints_cbor() helper producing { resource_allow: ["*"], .. },
  and pipe .constraints_cbor(&test_constraints_cbor()) into every
  token-builder chain in the test module

Scope: apple-notes, apple-reminders, email-generic, wecom, browser.
Cargo.lock updates reflect the ciborium dev-dep additions.

Tokens now conform to spec requirement C3.4 (tokens MUST include
constraints; default-deny).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
